title: pavetta subcapitata
intro: Pavetta subcapitata, also known as the West African shrub or wild jasmine, is a flowering plant species native to tropical Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and promoting digestive health. Pavetta subcapitata is typically prepared as a herbal tea or infused oil, which is used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
